name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-78
title: sync-check.py priority condition for additionalContext is wrongly silent on regenerate failure
description: hooks/sync-check.py lines 73-79: policy_section regex r'(?m)^.*rabbit-policy-start.*$.*?(?:^.*rabbit-policy-end.*$)' attempts to match a fenced policy section. If the markers are absent (current CLAUDE.md uses @-imports per generate-claude-md.py, no inline markers), policy_section returns ''. Then in the first-run/drift emit, additionalContext becomes 'expected' (the entire body of CLAUDE.md). That body contains @-imports but NO inline policy text — the imports are unprocessed strings. Claude receives the @-import lines as text, not as imports, so policy is NOT actually re-injected on drift. Either: (a) sync-check should read and expand the @-imports inline before emitting, OR (b) revise spec to acknowledge that drift correction relies on next-turn @ processing.

history.note: Fixed in Wave 2 PR. session-init R1 implementation restored (BUG-17); sync-check drift color now RED (BUG-34); tests use isolated temp-dir fixtures (BUG-40); scope-guard DENIES on unresolvable feature (BUG-43); @-import handling fixed (BUG-78). 9 new e2e tests; all 39 rabbit-cage suites pass.
